data.title: Danaher, Cryoport, Sartorius win Buy as Guggenheim expands bioprocessing coverage
data.description:
Guggenheim Partners expanded its coverage on the bioprocessing industry on Thursday, granting Buy recommendations for Danaher (NYSE:DHR), Cryoport (NASDAQ:CYRX), and Sartorius (OTCPK:SARTF) and a Neutral rating for Maravai LifeSciences (NASDAQ:MRVI).

Analyst Subbu Nambi projected a high-single-digit revenue growth for the industry over the next few quarters as post-COVID headwinds, including overcapacity and inventory destocking, subside along with concerns over the Biosecure Act.

Nambi also highlighted the bioprocessing industry’s growing importance, arguing that the FDA has so far approved more than 100 monoclonal antibodies, with its approvals accelerating over the past decade.

“Moving forward, we expect biologics to continue to account for a growing proportion of biopharmaceutical development pipelines and marketed products,” Nambi wrote.

Washington D.C.-based Danaher (NYSE:DHR) received a $275 target from Guggenheim due to its operating model, management, and cash position, which the analyst said would allow the company to generate inorganic growth despite a potential slowdown in core growth.

Calling Cryoport (NASDAQ:CYRX), a market leader in cryologistics, a key step in bioprocessing and biostorage supply chains, Nambi granted the stock an $11 price target, noting an underappreciated valuation.

Meanwhile, German-based Sartorius (OTCPK:SARTF) received a €249-per-share target, indicating a 19% premium to peers’ 2026E median EV/EBITDA multiple of 18.0x to reflect its near pure-play status and its exposure to high-growth bioprocessing.

Maravai (NASDAQ:MRVI), the San Diego, CA, developer of CleanCap mRNA technology, was the lone neutral-rated stock, with Guggenheim seeing more attractive pockets in the industry. The analyst argued that after pandemic-era advancements, the mRNA space, where Maravai (MRVI) is specialized, will take more time to expand.

data.title: Vertex downgraded to perform by Oppenheimer
data.description:
Oppenheimer has downgraded Vertex (NASDAQ:VRTX) to perform from outperform and removed its price target, citing mixed Phase 2 data for the company’s drug suzetrigine in the treatment of lumbosacral radiculopathy, or LSR.

The investment bank said it was lowering its rating “based on the uncertain benefit/risk profile of suzetrigine in LSR, which also casts doubt on its commercial potential in acute pain ahead of the Jan. 30, 2025, PDUFA date.”

Oppenheimer noted the company still has several upcoming catalysts, adding it continues to view Vertex (NASDAQ:VRTX) as the “industry leader in cystic fibrosis and rare diseases.”

data.title: Wall Street bounces back after worst day since early August as traders digest hawkish Fed
data.description:
Wall Street on Thursday staged a rebound after suffering its worst session in over three months. Market participants took some heart from data that showed strong U.S. economic growth, after having a night to mull over the Federal Reserve's hawkish interest rate cut.

The benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) was +0.67% at 5,911.40 points in afternoon trade, while the tech-heavy Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was +0.70% at 19,528.56 points. The blue-chip Dow (DJI) was +0.73% at 42,635.05 points.

Of the 11 S&P sectors, six were in the green, led by Utilities. Energy topped the losers.

Over in the fixed-income markets, Treasury yields were mixed. Longer-end maturities climbed, with the 10-year yield (US10Y) up 4 basis points to 4.56%. Shorter-end instruments slipped, with the 2-year yield (US2Y) down 5 basis points to 4.31%.

For more, see how Treasury yields have done across the curve on the Seeking Alpha bond page.

The Fed on Wednesday delivered a 25 basis point rate cut, as widely expected. But it was the central bank's updated Summary of Economic Projections, or dot plot, that was the story of the day. The Fed now sees higher inflation and fewer rate cuts in 2025, with chair Jerome Powell stressing caution going forward.

Markets reacted in an ugly manner, with the Nasdaq (COMP:IND) eventually sinking more than 3%.

The mood was much calmer on Thursday, and it was helped by a positive economic calendar.

Before the opening bell, the U.S. Bureau of Economic Analysis said real gross domestic product (GDP) improved at an annual rate of 3.1% in Q3 2024, a higher reading than the previous estimate of +2.8%. The update indicated that the U.S. economy remained robust, but was also another reminder that perhaps more easing from the Fed is not immediately required.

"GDP remains well above the pre-pandemic forecast. This reflects a combination of productivity being as expected and the labor force being much larger than expected - mostly due to immigration," Jason Furman, former deputy director of the U.S. National Economic Council, said on X (formerly Twitter).

"Overall (it) is hard to understand why the Fed cut rates yesterday in an economy growing at a 3% annual rate," Furman added.

At the same time as the GDP estimate, data from the U.S. Department of Labor showed that the number of Americans filing for initial jobless claims in the past week fell more than expected.

Also, in a notable development, the Conference Board's U.S. leading indicator index rose in November for the first time since February 2022. The index has long been seen as a recession bellwether, however its divergence over the last two years or so from a largely healthy economy had called that status into question.

Turning to Thursday's active stocks, Micron Technology (MU) slumped -16.1% and was the top percentage loser on the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND). The chipmaker's current quarter guidance disappointed investors, and so did its quarterly revenue decline in NAND flash memory.

The world's largest shoe company, Nike (NKE), and global economic bellwether FedEx (FDX) will be reporting results after the closing bell.

More on the markets

  • Did The Fed Just Trigger A Correction?
  • Stocks Have Swung From Overbought To Oversold Seemingly Overnight
  • Despite Fed's hawkish tone, Wells Fargo forecasts 'solid growth in 2025'
  • 25 U.S. ‘highest conviction’ stocks – UBS

data.title: Shell reaches deal with Argentina's YPF on liquefied gas project
data.description:
Shell (NYSE:SHEL) and Argentina's state-run energy company YPF signed an agreement to develop the country's liquefied natural gas project, the companies announced Thursday.
The $50 billion Argentina LNG project seeks to export the gas to major global markets.
As part of the agreement, the Dutch oil giant will participate in the first phase of the multi-stage project. It aims to produce 10 million metric tons of LNG a year.
Shares of Shell (SHEL) and YPF (NYSE:YPF) were little changed Thursday.

data.title: House GOP rushing to put together a new stopgap package to keep government running
data.description:
House Republicans on Thursday were rushing to piece together a new stopgap funding package to keep the government running past Friday after President-elect Donald Trump spiked a deal to keep agencies running through March 14.

“There’s a lot of moving parts,” House Majority Leader Steve Scalise, R- La., told reporters Thursday morning. “We’re going to try to get it done today.”

House Speaker Mike Johnson, R-La., was meeting behind closed doors with advisers to find a solution, The Wall Street Journal reported.

A revised package would need to get through the GOP-led House, and party leaders there have repeatedly needed help from Democrats to get spending bills through the chamber. However, Democrats appear to not be interested in helping after Trump and billionaire ally Elon Musk sank the bipartisan deal the sides agreed to earlier in the week.

Democrats say they will be bolstering Republicans’ tax-cut plans without an imminent deadline.

“Why would we help them pass the tax cut for billionaires and corporations?” Sen. Chris Murphy, D-Conn., was quoted as saying by Semafor. “The people I represent hate that idea.”

"Republicans must GET SMART and TOUGH. If Democrats threaten to shut down the government unless we give them everything they want, then CALL THEIR BLUFF," Trump and Vice President-elect J.D. Vance said in a joint statement posted on X.

Trump wants a stopgap funding bill passed by Republicans that includes disaster relief and aid for farmers, as well as the debt ceiling to be raised or eliminated. The ceiling must be regularly adjusted so the U.S. can pay creditors and avoid default.

The incoming president said Republicans need to reject Democratic “bells and whistles,” referring to provisions in the bill that include funds to rebuild the Francis Scott Key Bridge in Baltimore, new rules on pharmacy-benefit managers, and a trade deal with Haiti.

Trump threatened a primary challenge in 2026 for any GOP congressional member who opposed a debt-ceiling change.

He told NBC News in a telephone interview Thursday morning that eliminating the debt ceiling would be the “smartest thing it [Congress] could do. I would support that entirely.”

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

  • Congress extends funding deadline to March 14, secures $100B for disaster aid
  • Congress reaches short-term spending bill to avoid shutdown before election
  • SA Sentiment: What's the best way to address America's $35T debt load?

data.title: Savara starts rolling submission of Molbreevi BLA
data.description:
• Shares of Savara (NASDAQ:SVRA) were up 8% Thursday, the day after the company said it had started the FDA rolling submission process for a Biologics License Application for its drug Molbreevi for the treatment of the lung disease aPAP.
  • Savara (NASDAQ:SVRA) expects the submission to be completed by the end of Q1 2025. It also intends to request a priority review of the application.
  • APAP is a rare lung disease characterized by the abnormal build-up of surfactant in the alveoli of the lungs. Molbreevi was granted Fast Track and Breakthrough Therapy Designations in 2019 for the treatment of patients with the debilitating disease.

data.title: Kosmos Energy gains amid takeover speculation
data.description:
• Kosmos Energy (NYSE:KOS) rose 1.6% amid some takeover speculation.
  • Harbour Energy (OTCPK:PMOIF) is said to be looking at Kosmos Energy (KOS), according to traders, who cited a Betaville "uncooked" alert that circulated on Thursday.
  • The speculation comes after Kosmos (KOS) on Tuesday said it won't make a firm offer for London-listed Tullow Oil (OTCPK:TUWLF).

data.title: Regeneron reports positive results for two anti-coagulant drugs
data.description:
Regeneron (NASDAQ:REGN) reported positive Phase 2 data for its thrombosis drug candidates REGN7508 and REGN9933 and plans to initiate Phase 3 testing next year.

The company said REGN7508 is designed to maximize anticoagulant activity while minimizing bleeding risk. REGN9933 is aimed at providing an option for patients with the highest bleeding risk who are not candidates for currently available anticoagulants.

The Phase 2 studies evaluated REGN7508 and REGN9933 in the prevention of asymptomatic or symptomatic venous thromboembolism, or VTE, after unilateral total knee arthroplasty.

Regeneron (NASDAQ:REGN) said a pooled analysis showed REGN7508 was superior to enoxaparin and non-inferior to apixaban, and REGN9933 was non-inferior to enoxaparin, according to a statement.

Pfizer (PFE) and Bristol-Myers Squibb (BMY) market apixaban under the brand name Eliquis, while Sanofi (SNY) sells enoxaparin under the name Lovenox. The drugs are also available generically.

Regeneron (REGN) added there was no major bleeding or clinically relevant non-major bleeding in any arm of the studies. Likewise, no treatment-related serious adverse events were reported.

The company plans to begin Phase 3 testing of the drugs in 2025.

data.title: EU new car registrations fall 1.9% in November Y/Y
data.description:
In November 2024, new car registrations in the European Union decreased 1.9% Y/Y to 869,816 units, compared to a 1.1% rise in October 2024, the European Automobile Manufacturers’ Association showed.

So far in 2024, new car registrations remained stable (+0.4%) and almost reached 9.7 million units.

Negative results were seen across two of the region’s four major markets. Registrations fell in France (-12.7%) and Italy (-10.8%), while Germany was slightly stagnated (0.5%). On the contrary, Spanish market continued growth for the third consecutive month, rising 6.4% in November.

Registrations for battery electric vehicles declined by 9.5% to 130,757 units, with their market share falling to 15%, down from 16.3% last year. YTD market volume for new battery-electric cars was 5.4% lower than the same period last year, with the total market share now at 13.4%. The slump was primarily driven by decreases in registrations in Germany (-21.8%) and France (-24.4%).

Plug-in hybrid car registrations for November also declined by 8.8%.

Hybrid-electric registrations grew in November, rising by 18.5%. The market share now stands at 33.2%, up from 27.5% in November 2023, surpassing petrol car registrations for the third consecutive month.

Petrol car sales dropped by 7.8% and now represent 30.6% of the market, down from 32.5% the prior year. The diesel car market saw a decline of 15.3%, resulting in a 10.6% share.

For the month of November, market share leaders in Europe by registrations are Volkswagen Group (OTCPK:VLKAF) at 27.8%, Stellantis (STLA) at 14.3%, Renault Group (OTCPK:RNSDF) at 11.7%, Toyota Motor (NYSE:TM) at 8.40%, Hyundai (OTCPK:HYMTF) at 7.3%, BMW (BMWYY) at 7.2%, Mercedes-Benz (OTCPK:MBGAF) at 6.0%, Ford Motor (NYSE:F) at 2.8%, Volvo Cars (OTCPK:VLVLY) at 2.7%, Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) at 2.2%, Nissan (OTCPK:NSANY) at 1.6%, Suzuki (OTCPK:SZKMY) at 1.5%, SAIC Motor at 1.4%, and Mazda (OTCPK:MZDAY) at 1.2%.

The below pie chart depicts the company and its market share in November.

data.title: BofA cuts rating on Chinese internet firm Youdao to 'neutral'
data.description:
Bank of America downgraded their investment rating on Chinese internet company Youdao (NYSE:DAO) to "neutral" from "buy."

The research firm thinks the recent rally in share price is attributable to investor interest in the company's decent ad growth, which was driven by expansion into new verticals, strong RTA revenue growth, and application of AI technologies.

"We applaud Youdao's progress on ads business and margin improvement, but most positives could be largely priced in after recent strong rally, in our view; hence we downgrade the stock... We think both ads and education sectors could be reshaped by AI technologies over LT, though ST benefits could be insignificant," BofA said on Thursday.

BofA said while the smart device growth for the company could remain healthy ahead with the roll-out of new products, they view learning services momentum to be likely muted amid more prudent marketing spending.

They also expect growth to slow in Q4 with tough comps and less demand from e-commerce ads during the "Double 11" shopping festival this year.

DAO has a PT of $8.10, hiked from $7, implying an upside of over 11%. Shares are up 77.7% so far this year, while the benchmark S&P index rose 23%.
